texto: But one has to ask: why do the big things at such a high cost? I chose the quiet life. I like the idea of being recognized by my field and no one else. This way I have a purpose, one I believe in, but I'm not burdened by the constant idea of putting on a public image, a view of myself the masses can accept. Nobody cares whether I'm fat or cheat on my taxes. It is not the only right kind of life, of course, but it is the honest life for me. What I'm saying is, I make the right choices for myself.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Jaroslav Kalfar: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de sentido da existência e experiência vivida. O trecho “But one has to ask: why do the big things at such a high cost? I chose the quiet life. I like the…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que vida, fame, public-opinion pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de vida e public-opinion.

frase_original: But one has to ask: why do the big things at such a high cost? I chose the quiet life. I like the idea of being recognized by my field and no one else. This way I have a purpose, one I believe in, but I'm not burdened by the constant idea of putting on a public image, a view of myself the masses can accept. Nobody cares whether I'm fat or cheat on my taxes. It is not the only right kind of life, of course, but it is the honest life for me. What I'm saying is, I make the right choices for myself.

texto: Time was not a line, but an awareness. I was no longer a body, but a series of pieces whistling as they bonded. I felt every cell within me. I could count them, name them, kill them, and resurrect them. Within the core, I was a tower made of fossil fragments. I could be disassembled and reassembled. If only someone knew the correct pressure point, I would turn into a pile of elements running off to find another bond, like seasonal farmhands journeying from East to West.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Jaroslav Kalfar: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de passagem do tempo e prioridades. O trecho “Time was not a line, but an awareness. I was no longer a body, but a series of pieces whistling a…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que autoestima, body, self pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de viagem e self.

If only someone knew the correct pressure point, I would turn into a pile of elements running off to find another bond, like seasonal farmhands journeying from East to West.
